page: Amazon Web Services m5n.metal is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized bare-metal server. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The system features 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the m5n.metal demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, particularly in multi-threaded stress tests, software compilation, and ray tracing. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top tier for both cached small-block and uncached large-block read operations. Database and key-value store performance are notable, with strong results in Redis SET operations and PassMark database tests. However, single-core performance and text generation speeds for large language models are average to poor, indicating that the hardware is better optimized for parallelized throughput rather than sequential execution or complex generative AI inference.

The server features a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, making it highly suitable for network-intensive applications. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture offers high resource density and dedicated CPU allocation, eliminating virtualization overhead. This profile is optimal for large-scale enterprise applications, high-throughput web serving, database clusters, and parallelized CI/CD compilation pipelines that can leverage the high-speed network interface and multi-core architecture.
description: Amazon Web Services m5n.metal is a general-purpose, network-optimized bare-metal server powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 96 dedicated vCPUs across 48 physical cores at 3.1 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The server lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on its 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external storage and connectivity. Benchmarks show strong multi-core performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database operations, though single-core execution and LLM text generation are average to weak. This bare-metal configuration offers high resource density and eliminates virtualization overhead, making it qualitatively cost-effective for parallelized workloads. It is highly suited for high-throughput web serving, database clusters, and intensive CI/CD compilation pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.metal is a general-purpose bare-metal server featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-throughput database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.metal is a general-purpose bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-bandwidth bare-metal server featuring ninety-six dedicated virtual cores and one hundred gigabits per second network throughput for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM
High-speed 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for intensive data transfer
Strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth in benchmark testing
Bare-metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead for maximum resource density
Optimized for database clusters, high-throughput web serving, and CI/CD pipelines
